vulnerabilities.description: In Apache Tomcat 0.0.1 to 8.5.51, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.30, and 10.0.0.M1 to 10.0.0.M9, the HTTP/2 implementation did not limit the number of concurrent streams. This could result in a denial of service via resource exhaustion (excessive CPU or memory use).

In Apache Tomcat 0.0.1 to 8.5.51, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.30, and 10.0.0.M1 to 10.0.0.M9, the HTTP/2 implementation did not limit the number of concurrent streams. This could result in a denial of service via resource exhaustion (excessive CPU or memory use).

In Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.51, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.30, and 10.0.0.M1 to 10.0.0.M9, the default behavior of the HTTP/2 implementation has changed to be more strict when parsing HTTP headers. This could result in a denial of service if an attacker sends malformed HTTP/2 requests.

In Apache Tomcat 8.5.0 to 8.5.51, 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.30, and 10.0.0.M1 to 10.0.0.M9, the HTTP/2 implementation did not properly validate the length of HTTP/2 headers. This could result in a denial of service via resource exhaustion (excessive CPU or memory use).

Apache Tomcat 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.0.M23, 8.5.0 to 8.5.23, and 8.0.0.RC1 to 8.0.47 did not properly validate certain inputs, allowing remote attackers to perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attacks via a crafted URL.

Apache Tomcat 9.0.0.M1 to 9.0.17, 8.5.0 to 8.5.39, and 7.0.0 to 7.0.93 did not properly handle certain edge cases in the DBCP (Database Connection Pool) implementation, which could result in a denial of service via resource exhaustion.
